2605.12419.title: ORBIT: Preserving Foundational Language Capabilities in GenRetrieval via Origin-Regulated Merging
2605.12419.paper_summary: Despite the rapid advancements in large language model (LLM) development, fine-tuning them for specific tasks often results in the catastrophic forgetting of their general, language-based reasoning abilities. This work investigates and addresses this challenge in the context of the Generative Retrieval (GenRetrieval) task. During GenRetrieval fine-tuning, we find this forgetting occurs rapidly and correlates with the distance between the fine-tuned and original model parameters. Given these observations, we propose ORBIT, a novel approach that actively tracks the distance between fine-tuned and initial model weights, and uses a weight averaging strategy to constrain model drift during GenRetrieval fine-tuning when this inter-model distance exceeds a maximum threshold. Our results show that ORBIT retains substantial text and retrieval performance by outperforming both common continual learning baselines and related regularization methods that also employ weight averaging.
2605.12419.paper_summary_zh: 儘管大型語言模型（LLM）發展迅速，針對特定任務進行微調往往會導致其一般語言推理能力的災難性遺忘。這項工作在生成檢索（GenRetrieval）任務的背景下調查並解決了這一挑戰。在GenRetrieval的微調過程中，我們發現這種遺忘發生得很快，並且與微調模型和原始模型參數之間的距離相關。基於這些觀察，我們提出了ORBIT，一種新穎的方法，主動跟踪微調模型和初始模型權重之間的距離，並在這種模型間距離超過最大閾值時，使用權重平均策略來限制模型在GenRetrieval微調過程中的漂移。我們的結果顯示，ORBIT通過超越常見的持續學習基準和相關的正則化方法（這些方法也使用權重平均），保持了可觀的文本和檢索性能。
2605.12419.author: Neha Verma et.al.
2605.12419.authors: Neha Verma, Nikhil Mehta, Shao-Chuan Wang, Naijing Zhang, Alicia Tsai, Li Wei, Lukasz Heldt, Lichan Hong, Ed Chi, Xinyang Yi
